figures.caption: Figure 4: Representative BALM structural template dominated by default-mode regions. The accompanying behavioural associations are exploratory annotations and are reported descriptively rather than as confirmatory findings. Alt text: Brain-plot and behavioural-summary figure for a BALM structural template

Figure 3: Representative latent hub derived from ALMA. Under orthogonality constraints, the component merges default-mode and somatomotor regions into a single mixed template. Alt text: Brain-plot and behavioural-summary figure for an ALMA component, showing default-mode and somatomotor involvement and a descriptive association with Spatial Orientation.

Figure 2: Performance comparison illustrating the effect of structure-informed sparsity. Panels show template correlation for the coupled BALM and restricted decoupled models across true coupling strengths at 15%, 30%, and 50% target network densities. Error bars represent standard deviations over 5 replications. Alt text: Three-panel line plot showing template-recovery correlation as true coupling

Figure 1: Performance comparison between BALM, ALMA, and the tensor block model across varying Dirichlet concentration parameters (α). All reported metrics are averaged over 50 independent replications per setting. Panels show continuous-weight mean squared error, binary topology prediction, clustering stability and template recovery.

Figure 5: Representative BALM structural template with default-mode and limbic involvement. The small negative association with Picture Sequence Memory is used as exploratory annotation only. Alt text: Brain-plot and behavioural-summary figure for a BALM structural template
